title: Trickle timer integer overflow in interval_end calculation
description:
File: rust/lichen-rpl/src/trickle.rs
Lines: 121-123

The interval_end() method does not handle integer overflow:
```rust
pub fn interval_end(&self) -> u32 {
    self.interval_start + self.interval
}
```

Impact: Same as transmit_time overflow - if interval_start is close to u32::MAX, the addition will wrap around. The caller would see an interval_end that appears to be in the distant past, causing the interval to immediately expire and restart.

This would cause continuous rapid interval cycling, violating Trickle's exponential backoff behavior.

Fix: Use saturating_add:
```rust
pub fn interval_end(&self) -> u32 {
    self.interval_start.saturating_add(self.interval)
}
```
close_reason: Fixed: Changed interval_end() to use saturating_add() instead of plain addition, preventing wraparound when interval_start is close to u32::MAX. Added test to verify saturation behavior.
